publicationStmt.publisher: Die Schriftkultur des christlichen Äthiopiens und Eritreas: Eine multimediale Forschungsumgebung / Beta maṣāḥǝft

licence.p: This file is licensed under the Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike 4.0.

sourceDesc.p: Work of the literatures of Ethiopia and Eritrea

encodingDesc.p: Encoded according to the Beta maṣāḥǝft Guidelines. These Guidelines detail the TEI format ruled by the Beta maṣāḥǝft Schema. The present TEI file is enriched with an Xquery transformation taking advantage of the exist-db database instance where the data is stored and of the many external resources to which this data points to.

t:profileDesc.t:creation: The date post quem of the Arabic version () is provided by the death of patriarch Christodoulous, the date ante quem by a synodal letter from 1079 which is not included.
According to the work must have been translated to Ethiopic before 1577.

calendar.p: ʿĀmata ʿālam/ʿĀmata ʾəm-fəṭrat (Era of the World)
ʿĀmata śəggāwe (Era of the Incarnation – Ethiopian)

relation.name: betmas:isAuthorOfEthiopicTranslation
relation.t:desc:
Dictionary of the Amharic Language. In two parts. Amharic and English, and English and AmharicCharles WillamIsenbergLondonThe Church Missionary Society1841https://archive.org/details/cu31924026888481bm:Isenberg1841Dictionarybm:Isenberg1841Dictionary198 has attributed the translation to
Mäbaʾa Ṣǝyon , but according to Haymanotä abäwEAeEncyclopaedia AethiopicaAnaisWionEmmanuelFritschSiegbertUhligWiesbadenHarrassowitz Verlag2005II1073a-1075bbm:WionFritsch2005Haymanotabm:WionFritsch2005Haymanota1074 no evidence for this has been found in the manuscripts.

relation.name: saws:follows
relation.t:desc: The Maṣḥafa ṭomār very often follows Hāymānota ʾabaw and precedes the table of content.

relation.name: saws:follows
relation.t:desc: The Tǝmhǝrta ḫǝbuʾāt can be included at the beginning of the Hāymānota ʾabaw, following the introduction.

relation.name: saws:isAttributedToAuthor
relation.t:desc: The attribution in the text to Hippolytus is wrong.

t:bibl.title: Geschichte der christlichen arabischen Literatur. Zweiter Band: Die Schriftsteller bis zur Mitte des 15. Jahrhunderts
Studi e Testi
Letterature dell’Etiopia
Storia delle letterature d'Oriente
t:bibl.publisher: Casa editrice Dr. Francesco Vallardi società editrice libraria

t:bibl.title: Dictionary of the Amharic Language. In two parts. Amharic and English, and English and Amharic

t:div.t:label: Introduction
Tǝmhǝrta ḫǝbuʾāt
t:div.t:note: Most commonly, it is the version ʾƎlmasṭoʾagya, but it can possibly be also Tǝmhǝrta ḫǝbuʾāt (or a version which is difficult to define, Teaching of Mysteries (general record)).

Answer from Cosmas III, 58th patriarch of Alexandria to Basilius I, patriarch of Antioch

Synodal letter from Dionysius III, patriarch of Antioch to Mina II, patriarch of Alexandria
Synodal letter from Mina II, patriarch of Alexandria to John VI, patriarch of Antioch
Synodal letter from Dionysius III, patriarch of Antioch to Mina II, patriarch of Alexandria
Synodal letter from John VI, patriarch of Antioch
Synodal letter from John VI, patriarch of Antioch, to Mina II, patriarch of Alexandria
Another synodal letter from John VI, patriarch of Antioch, to Mina II, patriarch of Alexandria
Letters by Philotheos
Synodal letter from Philotheus, patriarch of Alexandria to Dionysius III (Athanasius IV of Antioch?), patriarch of Antioch
Synodal letter from Philotheus, patriarch of Alexandria to Athanasius IV of Antioch, patriarch of Antioch
Synodal letter from Athanasius IV of Antioch, patriarch of Antioch, to Philotheus, patriarch of Alexandria
Letters by John

Another synodal letter from John VII, patriarch of Antioch and the East to Zacharias, patriarch of Alexandria
Letters by Zakāryās
Answer from Zacharias, patriarch of Alexandria to John VII, patriarch of Antioch
Another Synodal letter from Zacharias, patriarch of Alexandria to John VII, patriarch of Antioch
Letters by Shenute
Synodal letter from Shenute II, patriarch of Alexandria to Dionysius IV, patriarch of Antioch (Answer to the latters letter from 752 Year of the Martyrs)
Another Synodal letter from Shenute II, patriarch of Alexandria to Dionysius IV, patriarch of Antioch (Answer to the latters letter from 751 Year of the Martyrs)
Synodal letter from Dionysius IV, patriarch of Antioch to Shenute II, patriarch of Alexandria
Letters by Christodoulous
Synodal letter from Christodoulous, patriarch of Alexandria to John VIII, patriarch of Antioch
Extracts from paschal letters by Christodoulous
Extract from the 16th paschal letter of Christodoulous
Extract from the 17th paschal letter
Extract from the 20th, 21st and 23rd paschal letter
Extract from the 21st and 23rd paschal letter
Extract from the 23rd paschal letter
Letters by John
Synodal letter from John VIII, patriarch of Antioch, to Christodoulous, patriarch of Alexandria
Another synodal letter from John VIII, patriarch of Antioch, to Christodoulous, patriarch of Alexandria
Another synodal letter from John VIII, patriarch of Antioch, to Christodoulous, patriarch of Alexandria
Letters by Ḥabīb
Extract from a letter of Ḥabīb b. Ḫidma ʾAbū Rāʾiṭa at-Takrītī
Extract from a letter of Ḥabīb b. Ḫidma ʾAbū Rāʾiṭa at-Takrītī
Extract from a letter of Ḥabīb b. Ḫidma ʾAbū Rāʾiṭa at-Takrītī
Extract from the letter from Yaḥyā b. ʿAdī against the doctrine of the Nestorians, addressed to ʾAbū l-Ḥasan al-Qasim b. Ḥabīb
12 Chapters by Gregory Thaumaturgus on the Incarnation
Six anathematisms by Julius, bishop of Rome
Five anthematisms by Vitalian, pope of Rome, quoted by Cyril in his Letter to the empresses
13 Anathematisms from Gregory of Nazianzus' Letter to Cledonius
The Twelve Chapters of Cyril, with declarations but without the prolegomena
Five anathematisms by Theodosios, bishop of Alexandria, from his letter written from exile to the Alexandrians
13 anathematisms by John of Burlos
Various testimonies on the Incarnation from Cyril of Alexandria, Gregory of Nazianzus, some books of the New Testament, the Pentateuch and the Psalms
Maṣḥafa ṭomār
t:div.t:desc: The Maṣḥafa ṭomār circulates also independently and is not present in all manuscripts containing the Hāymānota ʾabaw. It can also follow the table of contents.

t:div.t:label: Table of Contents
